seoTitle: Mount Mulgrave vs Mount Mulligan — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mount Mulgrave and Mount Mulligan. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mount Mulgrave vs Mount Mulligan: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mount Mulgrave and Mount Mulligan.
heading: Mount Mulgrave vs Mount Mulligan — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mount Mulgrave, Queensland and Mount Mulligan, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mount Mulligan has a lower overall climate risk score (28/100) compared to Mount Mulgrave (29/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mount Mulgrave or Mount Mulligan safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mount Mulgrave has a flood risk score of 59/100, while Mount Mulligan scores 46/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mount Mulgrave scores 17/100 for bushfire risk. Mount Mulligan scores 19/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mount Mulgrave or Mount Mulligan?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mount Mulligan has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area face minimal climate value impact based on current projections.
